facts.text: Arthur is the family’s oldest member, while Bernardo Jr. is the oldest still living.
A is the most common first initial in the family, with 10 names beginning with it, including Arthur, Amante, Arnold, Adrian, and Andrew. Can you name the rest?
March is one of the busiest birthday months in the family, with birthdays including Ryan, Jose, Leilani, Karylle, Lyla, and Luca.
The Barnachea family archive spans at least three generations.
Most Barnacheas in the archive are based in California.
Rudel's branch is one of the larger descendant groups.
Some Barnachea relatives share the same birth month across different generations.
The family includes both very young children and relatives over 70 years old.
From 12 children came 47 grandchildren and great-grandchildren — a legacy still growing.
At 16.85824° N, 120.38213° E lies the family’s provincial home in the northern part of La Union.
The most recent addition to the family is Elijah, born last February 2025.
Kara was the first of the grandchildren to tie the knot.
The Barnachea family has branched into surnames including Capinpin, Pope, Osgood, Legaspi, Murphy, Vo, Contreras, Cano, and Claros.
Mady is the newest legal driver in the family — rides are now officially available.
Males currently outnumber females in the family, 37 to 28.
There is a 19 to 9 ratio of male to female in the grandchildren generation.
Among the great-grandchildren, females now lead 12 to 7.
There are currently seven teenagers in the family. Stay strong everyone.
Arthur, Bernardo Sr., and Leopoldo were all born within just a few years of each other in the late 1940s.
Rudel, Amante, Reynante, Criselda, and Riza were born back-to-back in the 1950s, creating one of the tightest age clusters in the family.

A wave of cousins were born throughout the 1980s — Kara, Jason, and Vincent (1982); Ryan and Krystle (1983); Andrew, Arnold, and Tricia (1984); Adrian and Jeffrey (1986); and Brandon and Vanessa (1989).
Four generations of the family have lived concurrently.
Some households in the family include both parents and children listed in the same address.
Multiple family members live in California, making it one of the main hubs of the Barnachea family.
The Barnachea family includes members spread across several cities in Southern California.
There are multiple families within the Barnachea network with more than three children.
Some branches of the family have already reached the great-grandchild generation.
There are both very short and very long names across the family, showing a wide naming variety.
Several Barnachea family members have moved between cities while still staying within California.
The family includes multiple married couples where both individuals are listed in the archive.
Some households in the family include three generations living at the same address.
Several family members share the same first initial, making certain letters more common than others.
The Barnachea family includes both original family members and spouses who have joined over time.
There are clusters of birthdays that fall within the same week across different years.
Some cousins in the family are nearly the same age as their aunts and uncles.
The family has grown enough to include great-grandchildren across multiple branches.
There are multiple overlapping generations where siblings, children, and grandchildren are all alive at once.
Some Barnachea family members share both last names and middle names through different branches.
The family includes both long-established households and newly formed ones with young children.
Arthur is currently the oldest member in the Barnachea family archive.
Leilani is one of the earliest great-grandchildren in the family, marking the start of a new generation.
Rudel’s branch is one of the largest in the family, with four children and four great-grandchildren.
Several Barnachea siblings were born consecutively throughout the 1950s, forming a tight generational group.
A large number of cousins were born between 1982 and 1984, creating one of the biggest age clusters in the family.
Kara, Jason, and Vincent are all part of the same early-1980s cousin cluster.
Multiple family members share the same June birthday month, making it one of the most common in the family.
Krystle’s household includes one of the largest groups of children within a single family branch weighing in at 6 members and 1 dog.
Some Barnachea cousins are close in age to their aunts and uncles due to overlapping generations.
Several family households include both parents and children living at the same address.
The Barnachea family spans from members in their 80s down to children under 5 years old.
There are at least three active generations of Barnacheas represented in the current family archive.
Several family members share the same first initial, with certain letters appearing far more frequently than others.
California is the primary location for most Barnachea family members in the archive.
Multiple branches of the family are concentrated in Southern California cities like Carson, Cerritos, and North Hollywood.
Some households in the family include up to three generations living within the same home.
Several Barnachea family members share birthdays within days of each other across different years.
The transition from grandchildren to great-grandchildren has already begun across multiple branches.
The family includes both original Barnachea members and spouses who have joined across generations.
Some Barnachea relatives share both first and middle names across different generations.
The largest age gap in the family spans more than 70 years between the oldest and youngest members.
Those born in the 1980s—Kara, Vincent, Ryan, Krystle, Arnold, Tricia, Adrian, and Victoria—now have children of their own, forming the next generation.
The Barnachea family has grown to include great-grandchildren born as recently as the 2020s.
Some Barnachea households are centered around long-standing family homes that appear repeatedly in the archive.
Certain branches of the family show rapid growth across just one or two generations.
Victoria once downed a glass of 14-year-old Oban Scotch like a complete barbarian.
While visiting Victoria in college, Jason once spent over two hours drunk and lost in her apartment garage searching for Jon’s car — only to discover that it had been parked outside the entire time.
Anthony once owned a Snake Eyes G.I. Joe action figure — ultra rare and arguably one of the coolest in the lineup.

facts.template: It has been {years} since Kara and Tim got married.
It has been {years} since Krystle and Jose got married.
It has been {years} since Victoria and Jon got married.

facts.text: Jason’s favorite Filipino dish is sinigang—preferably loaded with extra tamarind and turned up extra spicy. He’s also a big fan of sisig for that chopped crispy pork explosion with a citrusy bite and an addictive fat-crunch balance.
Gerald’s favorite Filipino dishes are kare-kare, crispy pata, and beef sinigang.
Krystle’s favorite Filipino dishes are pork rib sinigang and tortang talong.
Jordan’s favorite Filipino dish is ampalaya, the bitter the better.
Tricia’s favorite Filipino dish is balut—no hesitation.
🧋 Leo loves his taho.
Oliver and his uncle Michael are currently in a height battle — it’s a close one.
